compiled_state.title: As a practitioner, I can run /dso:sprint on any ticket type and have it routed correctly
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Modify sprint Phase 1 to accept any ticket type as the primary ticket and implement the 3-layer clarity gate with type-appropriate routing.
**Why**: Core behavioral change — this is the walking skeleton that proves sprint works with non-epic tickets.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint accepts epic/story/task/bug; 3-layer clarity gate (heuristic → scope_certainty → user escalation); routing: epics → preplanning, non-epics → complexity eval → optional impl-plan; TRIVIAL non-epics execute directly; fallthrough on absent script and unavailable haiku; unrecognized scope_certainty treated as Low
- OUT: Bug-specific fix-bug routing (Story 4), threshold calibration (Story 5)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, /dso:sprint accepts epic, story, task, or bug ticket IDs and routes epics through /dso:preplanning and non-epics through complexity evaluation
  ← Satisfies: "/dso:sprint accepts epic, story, task, or bug ticket IDs as the primary ticket"
- When this story is complete, the 3-layer clarity gate runs in order: ticket-clarity-check.sh (Layer 1) → complexity evaluator scope_certainty (Layer 2) → user escalation (Layer 3), with graceful fallthrough when Layer 1 script is absent or Layer 2 haiku is unavailable
  ← Satisfies: "When the heuristic fails, the existing complexity evaluator's scope_certainty dimension serves as the second clarity gate"
- When this story is complete, Phase 1 no longer asserts type=epic; it accepts epic/story/task/bug types and routes accordingly
  ← Satisfies: "/dso:sprint accepts epic, story, task, or bug ticket IDs"
- When this story is complete, when /dso:sprint is invoked with a non-epic ticket ID, the sprint-list-epics.sh interactive selection flow is bypassed; when invoked without arguments, sprint-list-epics.sh continues to list epics only
  ← Satisfies: "/dso:sprint accepts epic, story, task, or bug ticket IDs"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Each clarity gate layer must be independently testable with deterministic inputs
- [Reliability] Verify fallthrough paths: absent script → Layer 2, unavailable haiku → Layer 3
- [Reliability] sprint-next-batch.sh implements a 3-tier hierarchy (epic → story → task). When the primary ticket is a story or task, this hierarchy assumption changes — routing logic must define what sprint-next-batch.sh receives for non-epic primary tickets
